summary: Converted `ftaction` from an asm object into a partially decompiled C source file and wired it into the text object build. The PR introduced many `ftAction_80071xxx`/`ftAction_80072xxx` C implementations or C/asm hybrid stubs, command dispatch tables, subaction-event parsing helpers, and supporting fighter/header type refinements. The reported match status for `src/melee/ft/ftaction.c` was 78.99% overall, with many small skip/advance handlers at 100% and larger handlers still partial.
what_changed: `asm/melee/ft/ftaction.s` was renamed to `src/melee/ft/ftaction.c` with about 60% similarity, keeping some inline asm while adding C implementations for numerous subaction command handlers.
`ftaction.c` added dispatch tables `ftAction_803C06E8` and `ftAction_803C07AC` of `FtCmd` function pointers plus a byte table `ftAction_803C0870`.
`ftaction.c` added MWERKS constants such as `ftAction_804D82A0`, `ftAction_804D82A8`, `ftAction_804D82B0`, and `ftAction_804D82C4 = F32_MAX`.
Several handlers use `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to preserve exact asm while also providing C equivalents outside that configuration.

`ftAction_80071028` was documented as a `GFXSpawn` subaction event and implemented by decoding bitfield words into bone, GFX id, offset, range, and flags before calling `ft_8009F834`; invisible fighters skip it via `ftAction_800711DC`.
Many trivial or skip handlers were expressed as pointer advances on `cmd->x8`, for example `ftAction_800711DC`, `ftAction_800715EC`, and one-word skip handlers.
`src/melee/ft/types.h` introduced `ftSubactionList` and `ftCmdScript`, replacing separate Fighter fields `x3E4`, `x3E8`, `x3EC`, `x3F0`, and filler with `x3E4_fighterCmdScript` plus adjusted filler.
`fighter.c` was updated to use named script fields such as `x3E4_fighterCmdScript.x3EC_scriptCurrent`, `x3F0_scriptLoopNum`, and `x3E4_scriptEventTimer` in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
`types.h` also refined layout around hitboxes by changing the `xDF4` filler into `HitCapsule xDF4[2]` and wrapped some fighter bitfields in byte/bitfield unions.
`inlines.h` added `gmScriptEventCast`, `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`, and `getCmdScript(Fighter*)` helpers, with a `@todo Fix naming` note for the script-event macros.
`lbcollision.h` exposed concrete `HitCapsule` integer fields `x24`, `x28`, and `x2C` instead of an opaque byte range, supporting hitbox command decompilation.
`gobj.h` moved several `HSD_GObj_804D78xx` extern declarations out of `gobj.c` into the header and changed its include guard style.
`src/dolphin/mtx/types.h` added `struct IntVec3`, and `forward.h` added/reordered the forward typedef.
`src/Runtime/platform.h` changed `F32_MAX` from a double literal to a float literal by adding the `f` suffix.
`tools/convert_data.py` changed the default `input_glob` from character asm files to `**/ft/ftaction.s`, likely to support extracting/converting ftaction data during this decomp effort.
smart_moves: Kept build progress incremental by switching `ftaction` to C while retaining exact inline asm under `MUST_MATCH && !WIP` for difficult functions.
Modeled the fighter command-script state as a named `ftCmdScript` substructure instead of continuing to use unrelated raw fields at `0x3E4` through `0x3F4`.
Added byte-plus-bitfield unions for fighter flags so code can match both whole-byte operations and named bit access patterns.
Used small local bitfield structs and `gmScriptEventCast`/`gmScriptEventUpdatePtr` macros to express packed subaction event decoding while preserving pointer-advance behavior.
Documented at least one command handler (`GFXSpawn`) with external HSDLib command-script field names, making future command decompilation more searchable.
Promoted needed prototypes and externs into headers rather than keeping them as local declarations, improving type availability for cross-file matching.
Fixed `F32_MAX` to be a float literal, which matters for generated load/constant behavior in float-heavy matching code.
Targeted the data conversion tool at `ftaction.s`, suggesting a focused workflow for moving embedded asm data/constants into C.
decomp_lessons: For large asm files, it is acceptable and useful to land a partial C conversion when the object can be built from C and remaining mismatches are isolated behind `MUST_MATCH` asm fallbacks.
Packed script-command formats benefit from typed bitfield views, but helper macro names should be treated as provisional when the PR itself marks them with `@todo Fix naming`.
When a sequence of raw struct fields is repeatedly used together, replacing it with a named substruct can unlock clearer code and reduce offset mistakes in callers.
Subaction handlers often consume fixed-width command words by incrementing `cmd->x8`; matching code should preserve the exact number and order of pointer advances.
Float literal suffixes can affect matching; constants intended as `f32` should use `f` suffixes when the original code expects single precision.
Whole-byte writes and bitfield writes may both be necessary for a struct field; anonymous unions can preserve matching flexibility.
Header cleanup can be required before decompiling a central file because action handlers touch fighter data, collision capsules, audio, commands, baselib GObj globals, and matrix/vector types.
naming_conventions: Command-script fields were named by offset and role: `x3E4_scriptEventTimer`, `x3E8_scriptFrameTimer`, `x3EC_scriptCurrent`, `x3F0_scriptLoopNum`, `x3F4_scriptReturn`.
Function pointer typedef for action commands: `typedef void (*FtCmd)(Fighter_GObj* gobj, FtCmdState* cmd);`.
Script-event helper names added: `gmScriptEventCast` and `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`, explicitly marked with `@todo Fix naming`.
Bitfield names retain offset/bit notation such as `x2218_b0` style fields and `allow_interrupt`, often inside a union with the raw byte field.
Function symbols remain address-based (`ftAction_80071028`, `ftAction_800711DC`, etc.) because semantic names are not yet known for most handlers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` blocks to choose between inline asm and C implementations.
Kept MWERKS inline asm syntax with `#pragma push`, `#pragma pop`, `nofralloc`, and `// clang-format off` for preserved assembly sections.
Converted asm labels from `.L_...` style to `lbl_...` inside inline asm functions.
Used `M2C_FIELD` and raw casts in some partial C handlers where struct layout or access width was still uncertain.
Added dummy unused stack arrays under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` in some C implementations to influence stack layout.
Used a temporary variable assignment pattern such as `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr((new_var = cmd)->x8, struct test1)` and comments like `permuter jank?` to shape code generation.
Maintained exact command pointer consumption by repeated `cmd->x8 += 4` statements instead of collapsing them into a single addition in skip handlers.
Introduced float scaling constants around `1 / 256.0f` and `0.003906f` for decoding fixed-point event data.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.

In-code review-style TODOs are present: `@todo Fix naming` for `gmScriptEventCast`/`gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`, `@todo Unused stack`, `@todo Suggests #Fighter::cmd_vars isn't an array`, and `@todo Unify with #ftCmdScript` in `lbcommand.h`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or replace `gmScriptEventCast` and `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`.
Find later PRs that fully match `src/melee/ft/ftaction.c` or remove the remaining inline asm blocks.
Search for follow-up changes to `ftCmdScript`, `CommandInfo`, or the `@todo Unify with #ftCmdScript` note.
Search for later semantic names for `ftAction_80071028` and other subaction command handlers.
Check whether `Fighter::cmd_vars` layout was changed after the `@todo Suggests #Fighter::cmd_vars isn't an array` note.
